blocks.body: Generic reference is a way of talking about a whole class / category of objects or persons. In English, you can use: the + adjective (the rich), a/an + singular (a tiger), or Ø + plural (people), depending on the context.

{ "title": "10. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "The + adjective = all people with this feature: the rich, the poor, the young (plural verb!).", "The + nationality (-ese, -sh, -ch) = all representatives of the nation: the Chinese, the British, the French. singular = the whole form in a very formal style (rare): The computer has changed our lives.", "Use the pattern in the examples.", "Use the pattern in the examples.", "Use the pattern in the examples.", "In general statements do not use the before the plural: Tigers (not the tigers).", "The rich are (plural verb), not is!", "Use the pattern in the examples." ] }
